preFix.title: Fitting a bathroom basin
preFix.subtitle: Fix a new basin to the wall or pedestal, connect the tap and waste, and commission the supply
preFix.excerpt: How to replace an old bathroom basin or install a new one. Covers fixing the basin to the wall or pedestal, fitting the tap and pop-up waste, connecting supply pipes, and commissioning without leaks.
preFix.sourceNotes: Collins Complete DIY Manual. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: Fitting a basin divides into two parts. The first is bench work: fitting the tap, waste, and overflow before the basin goes up. The second is in-situ work: fixing the basin and connecting the plumbing. Bench work is easier to do on the floor, where the underside of the basin is easy to reach.
Step 1, Isolate the supply and remove the old basin

Close the isolating valves under the basin or the main stopcock. Open the taps to drain pressure. Disconnect the supply pipes from the tap tails and the waste trap from the wall or floor outlet. Remove the backnut holding each old tap, then lift the basin free. Clean the wall fixings and note the supply positions, you will want the new basin centred on the same supply pipes.
Step 2, Fit tap and waste before mounting

With the new basin on the floor, fit the tap through the tap hole with its rubber seal beneath the tap body. Thread on the retaining nut below the basin and tighten with the basin wrench, firm but not over-torqued on a ceramic basin. Fit the

with a bed of plumber's putty or the silicone supplied by the manufacturer. Thread the waste backnut from below and tighten. Fit the overflow hose between the overflow hole in the basin and the waste outlet body if the basin has an integrated overflow.
Step 3, Fix the basin to the wall

Wall-hung basins need two heavy-duty rawlbolts or frame fixings into masonry, the basin must support a person leaning on it. Hold the basin against the wall at the correct height (typically 800 to 850 mm from floor to rim), mark and drill the fixing positions, and fit the wall brackets. Pedestal basins rest on the pedestal and need lighter wall screws. Use the torpedo level to check the basin is level before tightening the fixings. Apply a bead of silicone sealant where the back edge of the basin meets the tiled wall.
Connect flexible tap connectors (300 mm or 450 mm corrugated stainless) from the supply pipes to the

. Apply PTFE tape to any male-threaded connections. Tighten the compression nuts one and a quarter turns past hand-tight. Connect the waste trap to the waste outlet, standard 32 mm trap into the 40 mm wall socket with a push-fit reducer if needed. Restore the supply, open the taps, and check every connection for drips.

items.fix: Drain the basin, remove the waste, clean the seat, apply a fresh ring of putty, refit and tighten.
items.cause: Insufficient plumber's putty or silicone; backnut not tight
items.symptom: Waste leaks around the rim where it meets the basin

items.fix: Pack small plastic shims under the pedestal to level it. Confirm the basin is properly seated in the pedestal locating groove before fixing to the wall.
items.cause: Pedestal floor is uneven, or basin is not sitting square on the pedestal top

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
